data.body:
SCOPE AMENDMENT (brainstorm 2026-04-20)

This epic expands beyond the original ticket-delete scope to include two related surface-area changes:

1. NEW VERB SUGAR PATTERN
   - Establish the thin-shim verb pattern: new subcommands implemented as thin bash shims over a shared Python library (the sourceable ticket-lib API from Epic 78fc-3858). The delete verb being added here is the reference implementation of this pattern.
   - Verbs landing in OTHER epics (ticket ready in 2c28-e5b9; ticket claim and ticket messages in 0777-0f3d) will follow the pattern established here.
   - This epic does NOT implement ready/claim/messages — only establishes the shared-library-plus-thin-shim pattern via the delete verb.

2. NEW LINK RELATIONS: duplicates and supersedes
   - Add duplicates and supersedes as net-new link relation values (currently only blocks, depends_on, relates_to exist).
   - duplicates: directional link (source duplicates target); indicates source is a duplicate of target. Closing with resolution=Duplicate points at the target ticket.
   - supersedes: directional link (source supersedes target); indicates target is obsoleted by source. Used when scope expands, plans change, or a redesign replaces prior work.
   - Jira sync for both is lossy: both map to Jira Relates link type (Jira has no native duplicates/supersedes). Comments on the link or ticket explain the nature of the relationship.
   - Local-richer-wins precedence rule on INBOUND: if Jira has a Relates link and local has a supersedes link matching the same pair, the local supersedes value is preserved. Inbound Relates links without local match are imported as relates_to.
   - The actual Jira bridge sync work (Relates bidirectional with precedence rule) is implemented by Epic 83e2-04ae (bridge sync remediation) — this epic only adds the relation values to the local CLI and reducer.

Dependencies:
- Add depends_on on 78fc-3858 (sourceable ticket-lib API is the shared library this epic builds on).

RERUN REQUIRED: preplanning must be rerun for this epic to generate stories covering the new verbs and link relations. Complexity: COMPLEX (new relation values, new CLI surface, reducer schema change).
